perturbation.original_text: (c) "Products" shall mean casual apparel consisting of men's pants, shirts, sweaters, windshirts and raingear.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Products" from a specific list of items to simply "apparel," the contract now contradicts itself later in Section 2 where it specifies that Company may not use the Duval Identification in connection with any items for sale or resale, other than Company Products as specified herein. Originally, 'as specified herein' referred to the list in Section 1(c); now it's ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood that Company may not use the Duval Identification in connection with any items for sale or resale, other than Company Products as specified herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from a specific list of 'Products' to simply 'apparel' broadens the definition and directly contradicts the later clause. The original definition limited the items to which the 'Duval Identification' restriction applied. Now, the phrase 'as specified herein' in the contradicted text becomes ambiguous because 'herein' now refers to 'apparel' which is much broader. This creates a contradiction as the original intent was to limit the restriction to a specific list of products, not all apparel. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition where essential language is removed or altered, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: (f) "Outerwear" shall mean the golf shirts, sweaters, windshirts and raingear worn by Duval when he plays professional golf.
perturbation.changed_text: (f) "Outerwear" shall mean apparel worn by Duval when he participates in promotional events.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of "Outerwear" to apparel worn in promotional events instead of specifically golf attire creates a contradiction. Section 9(a) discusses affixing the Company Logo to Duval's Outerwear when he plays professional golf. The change in definition now makes this statement vague, because Section 9 mentions profesional golf, while this outerwear definition is for any promotional event.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) It is agreed that the logo or name of Company (or Company's subsidiaries as mutually agreed upon) (the "Company Logo") shall be affixed to mutually agreed upon locations of Duval's Outerwear, excluding the right sleeve which is reserved for one of Licensor's other sponsors, which he wears when he plays professional golf.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of "Outerwear" from apparel worn during professional golf to apparel worn during promotional events creates a contradiction with Section 9(a). Section 9(a) specifies that the Company Logo should be affixed to Duval's Outerwear when he plays professional golf. Since "Outerwear" is no longer exclusively associated with professional golf, the application of the Company Logo as outlined in Section 9(a) becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type as it removes the specific context of professional golf attire, creating conflict with the requirement in Section 9(a).

perturbation.original_text: 5. ADDITIONAL REMUNERATION. It is agreed that should Duval achieve any of the accomplishments set forth in the following schedule during the Contract Period, then Company will pay Licensor the additional remuneration set forth below for each such accomplishment due to the increased value in the Duval Identification. Accomplishment Additional Remuneration -------------- ----------------------- PGA Tour Win $25,000 PGA Tour 2-5 place finish $10,000 World Championship Event Win $40,000 World Championship Event 2-5 place finish $15,000 Major Win (Masters, U.S. Open, British Open, PGA Championship) $100,000 Major 2-5 place finish $25,000 Named to Ryder Cup or President's Cup Team $15,000 Player of the Year $40,000 All additional remuneration will be due within thirty (30) days of each such accomplishment. Company agrees that all additional remuneration earned by Licensor during each Contract Year will be added to the Annual Fee for each remaining Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. ADDITIONAL REMUNERATION. It is agreed that should Duval achieve certain accomplishments during the Contract Period, then Company will pay Licensor additional remuneration. All additional remuneration will be due within a reasonable time of each such accomplishment. Company agrees that all additional remuneration earned by Licensor during each Contract Year may be added to the Annual Fee for each remaining Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific schedule of accomplishments and associated remuneration, Section 5 creates an in-text contradiction because it now vaguely refers to 'certain accomplishments' without defining them. The requirement that 'all additional remuneration will be added to the Annual Fee' for the remaining contract years loses its definitiveness and enforceability, since there's no longer a specified amount associated with each accomplishment. This means the amount to be added to the Annual Fee is not clear, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. ADDITIONAL REMUNERATION. It is agreed that should Duval achieve certain accomplishments during the Contract Period, then Company will pay Licensor additional remuneration. All additional remuneration will be due within a reasonable time of each such accomplishment. Company agrees that all additional remuneration earned by Licensor during each Contract Year may be added to the Annual Fee for each remaining Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific schedule of accomplishments and their associated remuneration, directly contradicting the implicit understanding within the same section (as it's the contradicted text as well) that there's a defined and measurable way to determine 'additional remuneration.' This removal of specificity creates ambiguity and makes the section unenforceable, as the amount to be added to the Annual Fee is no longer clear. The original text provided specific accomplishments and remuneration, while the changed text makes the remuneration vague, thus creating an in-text contradiction based on Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
